tool_args.label: B46 Select Bus Service Update - CB1 Transportation Committee Meeting

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: B46 Limited to be upgraded to B46 SBS along Utica Avenue corridor in Brooklyn
Proposed B46 SBS replacing B46 Limited
B46 Limited generally travels between Kings Plaza and Williamsburg Bridge Plaza. Makes all local stops north of DeKalb Ave and south of Ave H.
B46 Local generally travels between Kings Plaza and DeKalb Ave
Utica Avenue B46 corridor identified as candidate for Select Bus Service in 2009 Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study. Second-busiest bus route in NYC.
Off-board fare collection for B46 SBS
Dedicated bus lanes with possible extension south of Church Avenue. Bus lanes implemented in 2014 in portion of corridor.
Transit signal priority for B46 SBS

Capital improvements at SBS stations including real-time passenger information, bus bulbs (sidewalk extensions at bus stops), pedestrian safety enhancements at complex intersections, new shelters, plantings, benches
First Community Advisory Committee meeting for Utica Avenue SBS outreach
First public workshop for Utica Avenue SBS outreach
Presentation to Community Board 8 Transportation Committee about Utica Avenue SBS
Second Community Advisory Committee meeting for Utica Avenue SBS outreach
Presentation to Community Board 3 Transportation Committee about Utica Avenue SBS
Presentation to Community Board 4 Transportation Committee about Utica Avenue SBS

Finalize service plan for B46 SBS and B46 local, develop conceptual street design changes south of Church Avenue and north of St John's Place, present to other Community Boards along corridor, Public Open House
Public Open House at Kingsbrook Jewish Medical Center Ballroom, 585 Schenectady Ave (b/w Rutland Rd & Winthrop St), Brooklyn, NY
Develop corridor plan, develop implementation plan
Second-busiest bus route in NYC, carrying nearly 50,000 passengers per day
